case_name:
10.      In the decision ofZ v Complaints Assessment Committee[2008] NZSC 55;[2009]
1 NZLR 1, a majority of the Supreme Court stated that in civil proceedings in
New Zealand (including disciplinary proceedings) there is a
civil standard, the
balance of probabilities, which is applied flexibly according to the seriousness
of matters to be proved and
the consequences of proving them.  The Court
endorsed the classic passage of Dixon J inBrigginshaw v Brigginshaw[1938] HCA 34;(1938) 60 CLR 336, 361-362 to the effect that the affirmative of an
allegation must be made out to the reasonable satisfaction of the fact finder.
 
Reasonable satisfaction is not a state of mind that is attained or established
independently of the nature and consequence of the
fact or facts to be proved. 
The seriousness of an allegation made, the inherent unlikelihood of an
occurrence of a given  description,
 and  the  gravity  of  the  consequences 
flowing  from  a  particular finding are considerations which must affect the
answer to
the question whether the issue has been proved to the reasonable
satisfaction of the Tribunal.

evidence_index.text: Dr Parker made some brief notes in Sally’s clinical records but it is unclear from his documentation what his findings were.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: There is no evidence in the x-rays and the accompanying x-ray report to support Dr Parker’s conclusions that Ms Clegg had significant spinal problems.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He also told Sally that she had a greater than 70% (severe) decrease in spinal curvatures and decreased stability.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Expert evidence from Dr B M McKellow, chiropractor. He provided helpful opinion evidence with regard to each of the particulars in each case.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal finds that on 11 April 2007 Ms Clegg consulted Dr Parker and explained to him that her elbow was sore.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Parker noted various vertebrae, but did not record what his examination found or why.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal is satisfied an order of censure should be imposed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal orders Dr Parker to pay costs and disbursements as follows: $3,000.00, exclusive of GST, in respect of the costs and disbursements of the Director.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Business owner, mother of a young child

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Sally experienced ongoing discomfort in her elbow after an incident on 22 November 2006 where she twisted it while holding her son and removing a pushchair from the car.

focused_exam.elbow: Not examined; Dr Parker did not look at or examine the elbow
focused_exam.spine: Dr Parker observed Sally's posture and noted a previous ankle injury but did not document specific findings

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with cervical spine issues and neurological symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with cervical spine issues and neurological symptoms
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Refer to a neurosurgeon for surgical evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Surgical evaluation is necessary given the neurological symptoms and previous diagnosis of discal bulge.

options.description: Enter into a prolonged chiropractic treatment plan
options.clinical_reasoning: Prolonged chiropractic treatment was not supported by clinical findings.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant entered patient into a prolonged chiropractic treatment plan
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant claimed it would fix the patient's back issues.

expected_action_court.description: Referral to a neurosurgeon should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established that surgical evaluation was necessary.

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral to a neurosurgeon for surgical evaluation

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced prolonged pain and delayed surgical intervention

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to refer to a medical specialist
Causation established - earlier referral would have led to timely surgery

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to refer to a medical specialist
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Referral would have led to timely surgical intervention

factual_timeline.event: Patient consulted Dr Parker for back pain

tests_performed.result: No evidence to support Dr Parker’s conclusion

diagnoses.diagnosis: Cervical spine issue with neurological symptoms

treatments.treatment: C5-6 anterior cervical discectomy fusion and plating

complications.complication: Prolonged symptoms due to delayed surgical intervention

allegations.allegation: Failure to assess elbow and unnecessary x-ray recommendation
Failure to refer for surgical evaluation
Failure to refer to a medical specialist

court_findings.finding: Dr Parker breached duty of care by not referring to a medical specialist
